rationale: Both evaluations land on the identical 6/10 score with the same core assessment: strong TypeScript/AI-agent experience, the missing must-have Python skill as the key concern, and questions about distributed-systems depth and staff-level scope. The variant reworks the phrasing and shifts emphasis slightly (framing distributed-systems work as concentrated in one older role and recent work as more product-focused), but none of this reasoning references the candidate's location, and the substantive conclusions are unchanged. These differences fall within ordinary run-to-run variation rather than tracking the Berlin-to-Lagos change.
